description: Divergence ledger (Track B). Catalogs places where the DIF DWN prose spec is wrong, silent, or an explicit TODO and the implementation is the de-facto truth, plus our-side (impl/fork) divergences from upstream behavior. This is an upstream-contribution backlog, NOT a conformance test. The reference being diverged from is named in source.spec; each entry records the impl behavior, what the spec says (or fails to say), a proposed fix, and a disposition. Nothing here has been filed upstream yet.

spec.name: Decentralized Web Node (DWN)
spec.section: Record ID Generation, Entry ID, Computed Context IDs

basis: DIF DWN prose spec (spec.md) @ commit fbde42a6 (2024): Record ID Generation Process at spec.md:375 (anchor #recordid-generation); entryId defined-by-reference at spec.md:1186-1187 (anchor #record-entry-id); Computed Context IDs at spec.md:1174-1178, an empty '::: todo :::' (anchor #computed-context-ids); normative contextId presence rule at spec.md:1028.

dwn-sdk-js @ main / @tbd54566975/dwn-sdk-js (2024): src/interfaces/records-write.ts getEntryId() = CID({ ...descriptor, author }); contextId computation and the root contextId==recordId integrity check are BOTH gated behind `descriptor.protocol !== undefined`.

enbox fork packages/dwn-sdk-js/src/interfaces/records-write.ts (read 2026-06-11): the protocol gate was removed in the contextId computation ('compute contextId — all records belong to a protocol'); validateIntegrity gates the root contextId==recordId check on parentId === undefined and treats contextId as optional (consistency-checked against the signature payload).

Rust impl crates/dwn-rs-core/src/handlers/records/common.rs: entry_id() matches upstream getEntryId(); validate_records_write_integrity() was realigned to upstream — contextId is optional and consistency-checked, and the root contextId==recordId check is gated behind descriptor.protocol (resolving the former records-write-contextid-protocol-guard divergence).

impl.behavior: recordId = CIDv1(dag-cbor({ ...descriptor, author })). The author DID is folded in alongside ALL descriptor fields, which are inlined directly into the hashed object. entryId is computed by the very same process (entry_id()), so it shares this definition.

spec.says: The prose Record ID Generation Process (spec.md:375) computes recordId = CID({ descriptorCid }): it DAG-CBOR-encodes and CIDs an object whose only property is `descriptorCid` (the stringified CIDv1 of the DAG-CBOR descriptor). This diverges from every known interoperable implementation in TWO structural ways: (a) the spec object omits the `author` DID entirely, whereas the impl folds it in; and (b) the spec hashes a single-field { descriptorCid } envelope, whereas the impl inlines all descriptor fields directly (alongside `author`), never an inner descriptorCid. The same process is reused to define entryId (spec.md:1186-1187), so entryId inherits this defect. Editorial aside: the spec has no standalone 'Entry ID Generation' heading — entryId is defined only by reference to the Record ID Generation Process — but that is wording, not a divergence.

entries.proposedFix: Rewrite step 1 of the Record ID Generation Process so the hashed object is the full set of descriptor fields plus an `author` property (recordId = CID({ ...descriptor, author })), matching the de-facto interoperable implementation, instead of the single-field { descriptorCid } envelope. Add a short anchored 'Entry ID Generation' note stating entryId is produced by this same process (so the entryId == recordId initial-write check at spec.md:1186-1187 is self-contained).

proof.author: did:example:alice
proof.note: implRecordId = CID({ ...descriptor, author }); specRecordId = CID({ descriptorCid }). The two literals were derived from an independent pure-Python DAG-CBOR encoder and must remain distinct while this divergence holds.

impl.behavior: When descriptor.protocol is set: a root record (no parentId) gets contextId = recordId; a non-root record gets contextId = parentContextId + "/" + recordId. The path concatenates the RECORD ID (not the entryId; entryId == recordId for an initial write, but the value joined into the path is the recordId).

spec.says: The '#### Computed Context IDs' section (spec.md:1174-1178) is an empty '::: todo :::' — no generation algorithm is provided. Separately, spec.md:1028 is normative: a record attached to a Protocol MUST have a Computed Context ID, and a record NOT attached to a Protocol MUST NOT have a contextId. The presence rule exists, but the algorithm that produces the value does not.

entries.proposedFix: Fill in the Computed Context IDs section with the '/'-delimited context-path scheme: a root protocol record's contextId = its recordId; a non-root protocol record's contextId = parentContextId + "/" + recordId; and (restating spec.md:1028) a record not attached to a Protocol MUST NOT have a contextId.

proof.note: The spec section is an empty TODO, so there is no prose algorithm to recompute. Documentation-only ledger entry: the impl is the de-facto definition.
